fields.name: Sales Cycle Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the sales cycle to which the stage belongs.

fields.name: Stage
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the stage within the sales cycle.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the description of the sales cycle stage.

fields.name: Completed %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of the sales cycle that has been completed when the opportunity reaches this stage.

fields.name: Activity Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the activity linked to this sales cycle stage (if there is one).

fields.name: Quote Required
fields.tooltip: Specifies that a quote is required at this stage before the opportunity can move to the next stage in the sales cycle.

fields.name: Allow Skip
fields.tooltip: Specifies that it is possible to skip this stage and move the opportunity to the next stage.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip: Specifies that comments exist for this sales cycle stage.

fields.name: No. of Opportunities
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of opportunities that are currently at this stage in the sales cycle. This field is not editable.

fields.name: Estimated Value (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the estimated value of all the open opportunities that are at this stage of the sales cycle. This field is not editable.

fields.name: Calcd. Current Value (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the calculated current value of all the open opportunities that are at this stage in the sales cycle. This field is not editable.

fields.name: Average No. of Days
fields.tooltip: Specifies the average number of days the opportunities have remained at this stage of the sales cycle. This field is not editable.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains the starting and ending dates of a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the Estimated Value (LCY) field shows the estimated value of the open opportunities that are created by using the sales cycle for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Date Formula
fields.tooltip: Specifies how dates for planned activities are calculated when you run the Opportunity - Details report.

fields.name: Chances of Success %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage of success that has been achieved when the opportunity reaches this stage.
